theme_and_archetype.description: A clean, scholarly, and editorial interface combining the trust of Nature.com with the functionality of Notion and LinkedIn. Uses left-aligned editorial typography, a restrained palette with a strong academic Oxford Blue accent, generous whitespace, and elegant information density. Avoids typical SaaS tropes (no purple gradients, no soft muddy shadows).

background.rules: Never use pure white #FFFFFF for the entire page background. Use a subtle paper-like off-white. Cards can be pure white to stand out.

accent.rules: Oxford Blue (#0F2847) is the primary CTA and active state color. Bordeaux/Crimson (#8A1538) can be used sparingly for critical alerts or specific reputation metrics.

hierarchy.h1: text-4xl sm:text-5xl lg:text-6xl font-serif text-slate-900 tracking-tight leading-none
hierarchy.h4_overline: text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.2em] text-slate-500 font-sans font-semibold

typography.alignment: Strictly left-aligned for an editorial feel. Center alignment should only be used for standalone quotes or specific isolated UI states.

layout_and_spacing.philosophy: Generous Spacing + High Information Density. Use large paddings (p-8, p-12) for outer containers, but keep tabular data and widgets compact.

grids.dashboard: grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-6. Use 'Control Room' dense metrics style within the editorial frame.
grids.discover_feed: grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-12 gap-8. Main feed spans col-span-8, sidebar/recommendations span col-span-4.

layout_and_spacing.containers: Max-width containers (max-w-7xl) mx-auto for general layouts. Articles/Project documents should be max-w-3xl for optimal reading length.

rules: Use sharp edges (rounded-none) or very subtle radii (rounded-sm) to maintain a serious, document-like feel.
Use thin 1px borders (border border-slate-200) to separate sections, mimicking newspaper columns or academic journals.
Avoid drop shadows. Rely on border contrast and background color shifts to define hierarchy. If shadow is absolutely necessary for a floating menu, use shadow-sm.
No glassmorphism. Use solid, opaque backgrounds for all panels and dialogs to ensure maximum readability.

component_instructions.general: Use pure HTML/Tailwind for marketing/landing pages. Use Shadcn UI for the dashboard, workspace, and interactive areas, but deeply customize them to fit the editorial theme.
component_instructions.buttons: Flat, sharp corners (rounded-none or rounded-sm). Primary button: bg-[#0F2847] text-white hover:bg-slate-800. Secondary: bg-transparent border border-slate-300 text-slate-900 hover:bg-slate-50.
component_instructions.cards: Cardless or very flat cards. border border-slate-200 bg-white p-6 rounded-sm. No shadows.
component_instructions.tables: Crucial for research OS. Use Shadcn tables. Left-align all columns except numerical metrics. border-b on rows, no vertical dividers.
component_instructions.tabs: Override Shadcn default tabs to look like editorial section headers (e.g., thick bottom border for active state, transparent background).
component_instructions.forms: Inputs should have sharp borders, minimal padding, focus:ring-1 focus:border-[#0F2847].
component_instructions.icons: lucide-react with strokeWidth={1.5} to match the crisp, elegant typography.

motion_and_interactions.hover_states: Text links should have an underline that appears on hover. Rows in tables should turn very light gray (bg-slate-50) on hover.
motion_and_interactions.transitions: transition-colors duration-200 ease-in-out. Avoid scaling transforms (no hover:scale-105) to keep the UI feeling stable and serious.
motion_and_interactions.loading: Use simple skeleton loaders or a thin top-of-page progress bar. No bouncy or playful spinners.

media_and_images.philosophy: Use authentic, high-quality photography representing academia, research, and data.

hero_background.description: Classic university library interior. Use as an editorial header image with a max-h-[40vh] and a slight black/30 overlay if text is placed directly on top.

profile_avatar_1.description: Male researcher portrait. Use for user profile placeholders.

profile_avatar_2.description: Female researcher presenting. Use for collaboration or team placeholders.

project_cover_data.description: Data analytics laptop dashboard. Use for project/workspace cover images.

testing_guidelines.requirements: All interactive elements (buttons, links, form inputs, tabs, menu items) and key informational elements (h-index, publication count, collaboration score) MUST include a `data-testid` attribute. Convention is kebab-case, role-based.
